merged_findings.finding: Phase 3's replacement-phase scheduler deliverable references `self.probe_scheduler.schedule(replacement_phase_id, model, expected_cell_count=...)` with a None guard, but Phase 3's Runner constructor only declares `scorer: Optional[ScorerProtocol] = None` — the `probe_scheduler` attribute is not added to Runner.__init__ until Phase 6 MODIFYs runner.py. Accessing `self.probe_scheduler` in Phase 3 code will raise AttributeError unless Phase 3 also initializes `self.probe_scheduler = None` in __init__, which is not specified in Phase 3's constructor signature or deliverables.

claude.finding: Phase 3's replacement-phase scheduler deliverable references `self.probe_scheduler.schedule(replacement_phase_id, model, expected_cell_count=...)` with a None guard, but Phase 3's Runner constructor only declares `scorer: Optional[ScorerProtocol] = None` — the `probe_scheduler` attribute is not added to Runner.__init__ until Phase 6 MODIFYs runner.py. Accessing `self.probe_scheduler` in Phase 3 code will raise AttributeError unless Phase 3 also initializes `self.probe_scheduler = None` in __init__, which is not specified in Phase 3's constructor signature or deliverables.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 6's run_session body extension computes probe-scheduler watermark as `expected_cell_count=len(config.selector.sub_studies) * config.selector.replications` reading from config.yaml, but Phase 3's run_session signature is `run_session(selector, replications, models, sub_studies)` — accepting CLI-resolved sub_studies as a runtime parameter. When a user passes `--sub-studies native` (overriding the config default of `native,neutral`), the probe scheduler registers expected_cell_count=6 (from config) while only 3 cells are actually dispatched, so the probe watermark is never reached and recognition probes silently never fire for the entire study.

claude.finding: Phase 6's run_session body extension computes probe-scheduler watermark as `expected_cell_count=len(config.selector.sub_studies) * config.selector.replications` reading from config.yaml, but Phase 3's run_session signature is `run_session(selector, replications, models, sub_studies)` — accepting CLI-resolved sub_studies as a runtime parameter. When a user passes `--sub-studies native` (overriding the config default of `native,neutral`), the probe scheduler registers expected_cell_count=6 (from config) while only 3 cells are actually dispatched, so the probe watermark is never reached and recognition probes silently never fire for the entire study.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 4 creates `openrouter_min.py` but does not specify that it defines `__all__`. Phase 6's contract test `test_openrouter_min_contract.py` asserts `set(openrouter_min.__all__) ⊆ set(dir(openrouter))` — if Phase 4 omits `__all__`, this test raises AttributeError at Phase 6 integration.

claude.finding: Phase 4 creates `openrouter_min.py` but does not specify that it defines `__all__`. Phase 6's contract test `test_openrouter_min_contract.py` asserts `set(openrouter_min.__all__) ⊆ set(dir(openrouter))` — if Phase 4 omits `__all__`, this test raises AttributeError at Phase 6 integration.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 3's runner.py constructor also needs `judge: Optional[...] = None` (same issue as probe_scheduler). Phase 6 says it 'extends Runner.__init__ with two new constructor-injected dependencies: judge and probe_scheduler', but Phase 3's run_cell body contains the judge-claim + judge-pair wiring that Phase 6 adds — the Phase 6 MODIFY to run_cell references `self.judge` which does not exist on the Phase 3-era Runner. While the MODIFY semantics mean Phase 6 adds the code, the constructor parameters must be present for the class to instantiate correctly if any Phase 3 test constructs Runner() after Phase 6 lands.

claude.finding: Phase 3's runner.py constructor also needs `judge: Optional[...] = None` (same issue as probe_scheduler). Phase 6 says it 'extends Runner.__init__ with two new constructor-injected dependencies: judge and probe_scheduler', but Phase 3's run_cell body contains the judge-claim + judge-pair wiring that Phase 6 adds — the Phase 6 MODIFY to run_cell references `self.judge` which does not exist on the Phase 3-era Runner. While the MODIFY semantics mean Phase 6 adds the code, the constructor parameters must be present for the class to instantiate correctly if any Phase 3 test constructs Runner() after Phase 6 lands.

merged_findings.finding: DroppedRow is shared across all concurrent sessions (no session_id field, by design), so if session A's paraphrase of phase X fails and redraw() writes a DroppedRow, a concurrent session B that already successfully paraphrased and is mid-dispatch for phase X will see `is_phase_dropped(phase_id)=True` at its next run_cell check and silently skip remaining cells for that phase. The session B cells that already completed are then filtered by Phase 7's aggregator as partial-completion, wasting cluster time and potentially dropping a valid phase from session B's analysis.

claude.finding: DroppedRow is shared across all concurrent sessions (no session_id field, by design), so if session A's paraphrase of phase X fails and redraw() writes a DroppedRow, a concurrent session B that already successfully paraphrased and is mid-dispatch for phase X will see `is_phase_dropped(phase_id)=True` at its next run_cell check and silently skip remaining cells for that phase. The session B cells that already completed are then filtered by Phase 7's aggregator as partial-completion, wasting cluster time and potentially dropping a valid phase from session B's analysis.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 2's selector.redraw() atomicity gap: it writes a DroppedRow to benchmark_registry.jsonl (under the registry fcntl.flock) AND an ExclusionRow to exclusions.jsonl (under the exclusions flock). These are two separate files with two separate locks. A process crash between the two writes leaves the system in an inconsistent state (DroppedRow exists but ExclusionRow doesn't, or vice versa). The plan claims both happen 'under a single fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) acquisition' but they target different files.

claude.finding: Phase 2's selector.redraw() atomicity gap: it writes a DroppedRow to benchmark_registry.jsonl (under the registry fcntl.flock) AND an ExclusionRow to exclusions.jsonl (under the exclusions flock). These are two separate files with two separate locks. A process crash between the two writes leaves the system in an inconsistent state (DroppedRow exists but ExclusionRow doesn't, or vice versa). The plan claims both happen 'under a single fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) acquisition' but they target different files.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 6's deblind classifier uses `StratifiedGroupKFold` which the plan claims was 'added in 0.24' — it was actually added in scikit-learn 1.0.0. Not a runtime issue since `requirements-benchmark.txt` pins `scikit-learn>=1.4`, but the provenance claim is incorrect and could mislead version-downgrade decisions.

claude.finding: Phase 6's deblind classifier uses `StratifiedGroupKFold` which the plan claims was 'added in 0.24' — it was actually added in scikit-learn 1.0.0. Not a runtime issue since `requirements-benchmark.txt` pins `scikit-learn>=1.4`, but the provenance claim is incorrect and could mislead version-downgrade decisions.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 9's `pilot_contention.py` accesses `CompletedRow.run_result.wall_clock_ms` — this path through a nested pydantic model requires that `CompletedRow.run_result` (the field name on the pydantic model) matches this exact attribute name. Phase 1's CompletedRow declaration lists the nested object types by class name (`RunResult`, `ObjectiveMetrics`) but never specifies the snake_case field names on the CompletedRow model itself. If the pydantic field is named `RunResult` (matching the type) rather than `run_result`, every access in pilot_contention.py and the aggregator breaks.

claude.finding: Phase 9's `pilot_contention.py` accesses `CompletedRow.run_result.wall_clock_ms` — this path through a nested pydantic model requires that `CompletedRow.run_result` (the field name on the pydantic model) matches this exact attribute name. Phase 1's CompletedRow declaration lists the nested object types by class name (`RunResult`, `ObjectiveMetrics`) but never specifies the snake_case field names on the CompletedRow model itself. If the pydantic field is named `RunResult` (matching the type) rather than `run_result`, every access in pilot_contention.py and the aggregator breaks.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 5's pre-run baseline guard calls `_detect_baseline_drift(baseline_freeze, current_freeze)` and triggers venv recreation on ANY difference including pure additions. But the baseline freeze is written once by `install-benchmark-deps.sh` after the initial install. If a legitimate project-level requirements change is committed between benchmark sessions (e.g. a new dep added to `requirements-api.txt`), every single run in the next session will trigger a forced venv recreation (~30s overhead × 360 runs = 3 hours of overhead) because the baseline was never updated to reflect the new commit state.

claude.finding: Phase 5's pre-run baseline guard calls `_detect_baseline_drift(baseline_freeze, current_freeze)` and triggers venv recreation on ANY difference including pure additions. But the baseline freeze is written once by `install-benchmark-deps.sh` after the initial install. If a legitimate project-level requirements change is committed between benchmark sessions (e.g. a new dep added to `requirements-api.txt`), every single run in the next session will trigger a forced venv recreation (~30s overhead × 360 runs = 3 hours of overhead) because the baseline was never updated to reflect the new commit state.

merged_findings.finding: No error handling specified for the case where `git clone --local --shared` fails because the parent repo's object store is being garbage-collected concurrently (e.g., a background `git gc --auto` triggered by another process). The shared-object alternates link means the clone's integrity depends on the parent's object store stability during the clone's lifetime.

claude.finding: No error handling specified for the case where `git clone --local --shared` fails because the parent repo's object store is being garbage-collected concurrently (e.g., a background `git gc --auto` triggered by another process). The shared-object alternates link means the clone's integrity depends on the parent's object store stability during the clone's lifetime.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 5's venv flock ceiling is 10 minutes, and the plan estimates worst-case contention at ~6.5 minutes. But with two concurrent orchestrators (documented supported state per Phase 1's concurrent-orchestrator contract), each running a venv-recreation + pytest sequence, the second orchestrator waits for the first's full sequence. If both trigger drift-recovery simultaneously, the second waits up to 10 minutes and may legitimately timeout with `install_failed` even though the underlying test would pass — a false negative scored outcome.

claude.finding: Phase 5's venv flock ceiling is 10 minutes, and the plan estimates worst-case contention at ~6.5 minutes. But with two concurrent orchestrators (documented supported state per Phase 1's concurrent-orchestrator contract), each running a venv-recreation + pytest sequence, the second orchestrator waits for the first's full sequence. If both trigger drift-recovery simultaneously, the second waits up to 10 minutes and may legitimately timeout with `install_failed` even though the underlying test would pass — a false negative scored outcome.

merged_findings.finding: The `lock: claim Phase N` git-log grep uses `--all` which includes remote-tracking branches and tags. Phase 2's lockres adds a reachability filter via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`, but the anchor_ref resolution falls back to HEAD when no upstream is configured. If the benchmark is run from a detached HEAD state (common after `git checkout <sha>`), the anchor is HEAD itself, and `--is-ancestor HEAD HEAD` is trivially true only for HEAD — every other commit would need to be an ancestor of the detached SHA, which excludes commits on branches merged after that SHA.

claude.finding: The `lock: claim Phase N` git-log grep uses `--all` which includes remote-tracking branches and tags. Phase 2's lockres adds a reachability filter via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`, but the anchor_ref resolution falls back to HEAD when no upstream is configured. If the benchmark is run from a detached HEAD state (common after `git checkout <sha>`), the anchor is HEAD itself, and `--is-ancestor HEAD HEAD` is trivially true only for HEAD — every other commit would need to be an ancestor of the detached SHA, which excludes commits on branches merged after that SHA.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 3 references `tests/benchmark/test_cli_run.py::test_models_flag_rejects_long_version_strings`, but `tests/benchmark/test_cli_run.py` is missing from both the project file tree and every phase Files list. The plan depends on a test file that does not exist.

codex.finding: Phase 3 references `tests/benchmark/test_cli_run.py::test_models_flag_rejects_long_version_strings`, but `tests/benchmark/test_cli_run.py` is missing from both the project file tree and every phase Files list. The plan depends on a test file that does not exist.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 6 wiring uses `Registry(config)` in `cli_run.py`, `cli_resume.py`, and `cli_probe.py`, but the Phase 1 `registry.py` contract never defines a `Registry` class or constructor. Only module-style APIs like `registry.claim(...)` / `registry.claim_judge(...)` are described. This is a fabricated API.

codex.finding: Phase 6 wiring uses `Registry(config)` in `cli_run.py`, `cli_resume.py`, and `cli_probe.py`, but the Phase 1 `registry.py` contract never defines a `Registry` class or constructor. Only module-style APIs like `registry.claim(...)` / `registry.claim_judge(...)` are described. This is a fabricated API.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 3/6 scheduler logic calls `len(config.selector.sub_studies) * config.selector.replications` inside `Runner.run_session()` and replacement-phase scheduling, but no `config` object is part of the declared `Runner` constructor or `run_session` signature anywhere in the plan. The execution path depends on an undeclared variable.

codex.finding: Phase 3/6 scheduler logic calls `len(config.selector.sub_studies) * config.selector.replications` inside `Runner.run_session()` and replacement-phase scheduling, but no `config` object is part of the declared `Runner` constructor or `run_session` signature anywhere in the plan. The execution path depends on an undeclared variable.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 5 later relies on a helper `runner._reinstall_contestant_requirements(clone_path: Path)`, but that method is not included in the explicit Phase 5 `Runner` method list and is absent from the cited code/contracts. The plan invokes an undeclared helper on a critical install path.

codex.finding: Phase 5 later relies on a helper `runner._reinstall_contestant_requirements(clone_path: Path)`, but that method is not included in the explicit Phase 5 `Runner` method list and is absent from the cited code/contracts. The plan invokes an undeclared helper on a critical install path.

merged_findings.finding: The plan hardcodes external OpenRouter model identifiers such as `openai/gpt-5`, `google/gemini-2.5-pro`, `meta-llama/llama-3.1-405b-instruct`, `mistralai/mixtral-8x22b-instruct`, and `deepseek/deepseek-chat` without any cited proof that those exact slugs are valid in the target account/environment. If any slug is wrong, preflight fails before the benchmark can start.

codex.finding: The plan hardcodes external OpenRouter model identifiers such as `openai/gpt-5`, `google/gemini-2.5-pro`, `meta-llama/llama-3.1-405b-instruct`, `mistralai/mixtral-8x22b-instruct`, and `deepseek/deepseek-chat` without any cited proof that those exact slugs are valid in the target account/environment. If any slug is wrong, preflight fails before the benchmark can start.

merged_findings.finding: Walter and Lomax 'enrichment' snapshots are recorded as regression covariates even though the plan explicitly says they reflect each service's own main-branch/workspace health, not the contestant's commit. That injects unrelated cluster state into the model as if it were run-specific evidence.

codex.finding: Walter and Lomax 'enrichment' snapshots are recorded as regression covariates even though the plan explicitly says they reflect each service's own main-branch/workspace health, not the contestant's commit. That injects unrelated cluster state into the model as if it were run-specific evidence.
